araṇyāni or araṇyānī, f. a desert, large forest,  RV. x, 146, 4 ;  AV.  &c.
the goddess of the wilderness and desert,  RV. x, 146, 1 -6.

results.rendered_html:
araṇyāniḥ -nī f. [araṇya-ānuk, ṅīp ca;  P. IV. 1. 49 ; himāraṇyayormahattve]
1 A large forest or desert, vast wilderness; yathāraṇyānyāmutsāścaraṃtaḥ  Śat. Br. 
2 The spirit or presiding deity of the woods and mother of wild animals.
